# Scope of Work Template — Commercial Hotel
**Project type:** `commercial_hotel`
**Use for:** Hotel, boutique hotel, resort, or hospitality lodging projects subject to SECTUR and COFEPRIS regulations.

---

## Key Distinctions for Hotel Projects

Hotel projects differ from residential in the following critical ways:
1. **Regulatory compliance:** SECTUR (Secretaría de Turismo) classification requirements apply; COFEPRIS food and beverage regulations apply if F&B program is included
2. **Brand standards:** If the property operates under a hotel brand or franchise, the brand's design standards manual governs all interior and exterior design choices
3. **FF&E scope boundary:** Oficio Taller specifies FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures, and Equipment); procurement is the client's responsibility unless explicitly included as an added service
4. **MEP complexity:** HVAC is always required for hotel projects; AV (audio/visual) systems are always required; fire suppression system design is required
5. **Program complexity:** Guest rooms, public spaces, back-of-house, F&B, spa, pool, and conference areas each require separate program sheets

---

## Phase Structure

### Phase 1 — Estudios Previos y Conceptual (Preliminary Studies & Conceptual Design)
**Deliverables:**
- Parti diagram and design narrative (2–4 pages for hotel; includes guest experience narrative)
- Preliminary floor plan sketches by functional zone (guest room floors, public areas, back-of-house, site)
- Site placement / orientation / massing concept
- 3D conceptual massing model
- Material, finish, and brand direction board (preliminary; if brand-affiliated, aligned with brand standards)
- Space arrangement diagram showing all programmed zones (guestrooms, lobby, F&B, spa, pool, conference, BOH)
- SECTUR classification target confirmed with client (1–5 stars or boutique category)

**Collaborators required:** None at this phase. All engineers and specialty consultants join Phase 2.

**Revision assumptions:** 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 15,000/day.

**Milestone trigger:** Phase 1 complete when client provides written approval of conceptual direction. If brand-affiliated, brand concept approval required before client milestone.

---

### Phase 2 — Anteproyecto / Concepto (Schematic Design)
**Deliverables:**
- Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale) — all functional zones
- Elevations all facades (1:100)
- Building sections minimum 4 (1:100)
- Roof plan
- Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, access, service entry, parking, landscape concept areas
- Interior design criteria document by zone (lobby, corridors, guest rooms, F&B, spa, pool, BOH)
- Guest room typical unit plan with furniture layout (standard, accessible, suite variants)
- Window and door schedule
- Preliminary structural coordination sketch
- SECTUR compliance checklist (room count, accessible room percentage, egress requirements, signage zones)
- COFEPRIS F&B zone compliance checklist (if F&B program included: kitchen ventilation, grease trap, NSF/health code layout)
- Brand standards compliance confirmation (if brand-affiliated: document confirming design aligns with brand manual)

**Collaborators required:**
- Structural engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates; client contract is separate)
- Electrical engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- Plumbing / hydraulic engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- HVAC engineer — always required for hotel projects (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- AV / technology consultant — always required for hotel projects (Oficio Taller coordinates)

**Revision assumptions:** 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 15,000/day.

**Milestone trigger:** Phase 2 complete when client provides written approval. If brand-affiliated, brand approval required.

---

### Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination (Coordinación de Ingeniería)
**Deliverables:**
- Structural engineering drawings integrated with architectural set
- Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules (including generator and UPS systems for hotel operations)
- Lighting layout plan — all zones — with fixture specifications (design-led lighting for guest experience zones)
- HVAC system design — always required: zoned system layout, equipment schedules, energy efficiency targets
- Plumbing and hydraulic layout (including pool systems if applicable)
- Fire suppression system design (sprinkler layout and design basis)
- AV / technology infrastructure layout — always required: in-room AV, public area AV, conference AV, network backbone
- Landscape architecture: excluded by default (client responsibility for independent landscape architect). If requested as added service, add to Phase 2 deliverables and this phase.
- Irrigation plan: excluded by default for hotel (resort-scale irrigation is a specialist scope — client responsibility). Included if client requests as added service.
- Solar systems: excluded by default unless client confirms interest before Phase 2 completion.
- Foundation plan and details

**Collaborators required:**
- All engineers from Phase 2 must deliver coordinated drawings
- Fire protection engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
- AV/technology consultant (Oficio Taller coordinates)

**Revision assumptions:** 1 round of coordination revision included. Program additions after Phase 2 approval are billed as change orders.

---

### Phase 4 — Executive Plans (Planos Ejecutivos)
**Deliverables:**
- Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP + HVAC + fire + AV)
- Detail sheets: wall sections, ceiling details, lobby feature elements, guest room millwork, openings
- Finish schedule (all surfaces, all zones, all room types)
- Door and window schedule with hardware and acoustic specifications
- FF&E specification document: room-by-room specification of all furniture, fixtures, and equipment (procurement is client's responsibility unless added service)
- Built-in millwork drawings (reception desk, bar, case goods prototypes for guest rooms)
- Technical specifications document
- SECTUR permit-ready drawing package

**Collaborators required:** All engineers from Phase 3 must deliver coordinated final drawings.

**Revision assumptions:** 1 round of coordination revision included. Client changes after executive plan approval require a change order.

---

### Phase 5 — Contractor Bidding (Licitación)
**Deliverables:**
- Bid package preparation (executive plans + spec document sent to minimum 3 contractors — hotel projects require broader competition)
- Bid comparison matrix (contractor name, total, key line items, timeline, experience with hospitality projects, notes)
- Recommendation with rationale (including contractor hospitality project experience assessment)

**Standard requirement:** Minimum 3 bids required for hotel projects (vs. 2 for residential). If fewer than 3 respond, escalated to client.

**Local contractor cost validation:** Bid prices reviewed against current hospitality construction cost benchmarks (MXN per sqm for hotel category and quality tier). Significant deviations are flagged with explanation before recommending a contractor.

---

### Phase 6 — Permitting (Trámites)
**Deliverables:**
- Building permit application package (architectural + structural + MEP)
- SECTUR hotel classification permit package
- COFEPRIS F&B permit package (if F&B program included)
- Submission to all relevant authorities
- Tracking and correction management until all permits issued

**Jurisdiction:** Determined by project location (Municipio de [city] + SECTUR delegación + COFEPRIS jurisdiction if applicable).

---

### Phase 7 — Construction Administration (Supervisión Arquitectónica)
**Status:** Optional — included only if client adds supervision scope. Strongly recommended for hotel projects given complexity.
**Deliverables (if included):**
- Weekly site visit reports with photo documentation
- Material, finish, and FF&E mock-up approval sign-offs
- RFI responses to contractor (hotel projects generate high RFI volume — budget accordingly)
- Guest room prototype review and sign-off before full-floor production
- Construction milestone confirmation for invoice triggers
- Pre-opening punch list and corrections tracking

---

## Landscape, Irrigation, Solar Scope

- **Landscape architecture:** Excluded by default. Client responsibility for independent landscape architect. If requested as an added service, add to Phase 2 and Phase 3 deliverables.
- **Irrigation:** Excluded by default for hotel (resort-scale irrigation is a specialist scope). Included only if client explicitly requests as an added service.
- **Solar systems:** Excluded by default. Included only if client confirms interest before Phase 2 completion.

---

## Local Contractor Cost Validation

Included in Phase 5: bid prices reviewed against hospitality construction cost benchmarks (MXN per sqm for hotel category) before recommendation. Minimum 3 bids required.

---

## Revision Assumptions

- Phase 1 — Conceptual Design: 2 rounds included. Each additional round: MXN 15,000/day.
- Phase 2 — Architectural Design: 2 rounds included. Brand-standard-driven revisions beyond 2 rounds billed as change orders (MXN 15,000/day).
- Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination: 1 round included.
- Phase 4 — Executive Plans: 1 round included. Client-initiated changes after Phase 4 approval require a change order.

---

## Typical Timeline

| Phase | Typical Duration |
|---|---|
| Phase 1 — Conceptual Design | 5–8 weeks |
| Phase 2 — Architectural Design | 10–16 weeks |
| Phase 3 — Engineering Coordination | 6–10 weeks |
| Phase 4 — Executive Plans | 6–10 weeks |
| Phase 5 — Contractor Bidding | 4–6 weeks |
| Phase 6 — Permitting | 8–24 weeks (multi-authority; SECTUR processing time varies) |
| Phase 7 — Construction Administration | Duration of construction (hotel: 16–24 months typical) |
| **Total design phases (excl. permitting + construction)** | 31–50 weeks |

Note: Brand approval processes can add 4–8 weeks. Durations are typical estimates. Actual timeline set by Pablo in `project-schedule.json`.

---

## Payment Schedule

| Milestone | Name | Percentage | Amount (TBD) | Trigger Event |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | Contract Signing | 25% | — | Signed SOW + first payment received |
| M2 | Concept Design Approved | 15% | — | Client written approval of Phase 1 (and brand approval if applicable) |
| M3 | Architectural Design Approved | 20% | — | Client written approval of Phase 2 architectural drawings |
| M4 | Construction Documents Delivered | 25% | — | Executive plans package delivered to client |
| M5 | Permits Obtained | 15% | — | All required permits (building + SECTUR + COFEPRIS if applicable) delivered to client |

Note: Hotel payment schedule differs from residential — additional milestone at Phase 2 approval reflects greater design effort.

Amounts are calculated by Bruno using `cost-basis.json` total_estimate × percentages above.
Payment currency: MXN unless otherwise agreed in writing.
Payment method: Bank transfer to Oficio Taller CLABE account (provided in invoice).

---

## Responsibilities Matrix

| Deliverable | Responsible Party | Reviewed by |
|---|---|---|
| Conceptual design | Oficio Taller | Client (+ brand if applicable) |
| Architectural drawings | Oficio Taller | Client (+ brand if applicable) |
| SECTUR compliance package | Oficio Taller | SECTUR / Client |
| COFEPRIS F&B compliance | Oficio Taller | COFEPRIS / Client |
| Brand standards compliance | Oficio Taller | Brand design team |
| Structural calculations | Structural engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| MEP engineering | MEP engineers | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| HVAC engineering | HVAC engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| AV / technology design | AV consultant | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| Fire protection design | Fire protection engineer | Oficio Taller coordinates |
| FF&E specification | Oficio Taller | Client approves |
| FF&E procurement | Client | — |
| Executive plans set | Oficio Taller | Client + engineers |
| Bid package | Oficio Taller | Client selects contractor |
| Permit applications | Oficio Taller | Multiple authorities |
| Construction | Contractor | Oficio Taller (if supervision in scope) |
| Payment milestone sign-off | Client | Oficio Taller invoices |

---

## Standard Exclusions

The following are explicitly **not included** in this scope:
1. Structural, electrical, plumbing, civil, HVAC, AV, and fire protection engineering fees (client contracts engineers directly; Oficio Taller coordinates only)
2. Permit fees, SECTUR fees, COFEPRIS fees, municipal taxes, or government charges
3. Contractor selection or construction contracts (Oficio Taller recommends; client decides)
4. FF&E procurement (Oficio Taller specifies; client procures)
5. OS&E (Operating Supplies and Equipment) specification or procurement
6. Topographic survey (required — client responsibility to provide)
7. Hydrologic or soil study (client responsibility)
8. Legal review of property title or easements
9. Construction cost guarantees
10. Brand franchise fees or brand management agreements
11. Hotel operating system (PMS, POS) specification or procurement
12. Signage and wayfinding (excluded by default; add as service if requested)
13. Pre-opening services (staffing, training, soft opening management)

---

## Project-Type Clauses

```json
{
  "project_type_clauses": [
    {
      "clause": "hospitality_compliance",
      "title": "SECTUR and COFEPRIS Regulatory Compliance",
      "text": "This project is subject to SECTUR (Secretaría de Turismo) hotel classification regulations and, where the program includes food and beverage service, COFEPRIS health and food safety regulations. Oficio Taller will prepare compliance documentation and permit packages for both authorities. Processing timelines set by SECTUR and COFEPRIS are outside Oficio Taller's control and do not constitute project delays attributable to Oficio Taller. Client is responsible for ongoing SECTUR classification renewals after project completion."
    },
    {
      "clause": "brand_standards_coordination",
      "title": "Brand Design Standards Coordination",
      "text": "If this project operates under a hotel brand, franchise agreement, or soft-brand affiliation, the brand's design standards manual governs all exterior and interior design decisions within the brand's purview. Oficio Taller will design in compliance with the applicable brand standards manual provided by the client. The client is responsible for providing the current brand standards manual before Phase 1 begins. Design changes required by brand approval beyond 2 revision rounds will be billed as change orders. Brand review timelines are outside Oficio Taller's control."
    },
    {
      "clause": "ffe_scope_boundary",
      "title": "FF&E Specification vs. Procurement Boundary",
      "text": "Oficio Taller's scope includes the specification of all Furniture, Fixtures, and Equipment (FF&E) — defining item types, quality standards, finishes, and placement. FF&E procurement (purchasing, vendor management, delivery coordination, and installation) is the client's responsibility unless explicitly added as a service in writing. Oficio Taller is not responsible for FF&E budget overruns arising from market pricing at time of procurement."
    },
    {
      "clause": "esignature",
      "title": "Electronic Signature",
      "text": "This document is valid when signed electronically by the client and an authorized Oficio Taller representative. Electronic signatures carry the same legal weight as physical signatures under Mexican federal law (NOM-151-SCFI)."
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Tomás Instructions

When producing `scope-of-work.json` from this template:
1. Read `area-program.json` to determine actual program size, room count, and functional zones
2. Read `cost-basis.json` to populate payment milestone amounts (M1–M5 × percentages above — note hotel percentages differ from residential)
3. Set `project_type_clauses` from the JSON block above verbatim (all four clauses required)
4. Confirm with seed data whether project is brand-affiliated; if yes, note brand standards requirement in Phase 1 and Phase 2
5. Confirm whether F&B program is included; if yes, activate COFEPRIS checklist in Phase 2 and COFEPRIS permit in Phase 6
6. HVAC engineering is always required — do not mark as excluded
7. AV / technology is always required — do not mark as excluded
8. Set `revision_assumptions` per phase as specified above (hotel rates: MXN 15,000/day)
9. Do NOT include supervision (Phase 7) unless state.json or seed data explicitly includes it — but note in scope that it is strongly recommended
10. Set minimum bid count to 3 (not 2) in Phase 5
